comment: Premise-level source/provenance audit. Each audit-premise from Assumptions.lean is judged individually; group-level approval is not sufficient.

assumption_admissible_combinatorial_repo.reason: Definition 3.2 states truthfulness over bidder types and vectors of declarations; the Lean domain parameter is the finite formalization of the declaration/type domain over which truthfulness is asserted.

admissible : CombinatorialReport Bidder.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 256-272: truthfulness is quantified over true type `t` and any vector `D` of declarations.
admissible : CombinatorialReport Bidder.reason: The source's declaration/type quantification is represented by the Lean admissible declaration-domain parameter.

assumption_external_exact_set_packing_co.reason: The source theorem cites Karp/Hastad hardness and polynomial-time preservation facts; the current Lean library records these as the public partial-formalization boundary rather than deriving machine-level NP-hardness, so this is not a source caveat.
assumption_external_exact_set_packing_co.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 510-525, Theorem 6.1 and cited Karp/Hastad route

hexternal : ∀ wspSolver, weightedSetPack.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 510-525: exact allocation is NP-hard and the approximation consequence would imply `NP = ZPP`.
hexternal : ∀ wspSolver, weightedSetPack.reason: The source states the complexity consequence, but the current Lean library does not derive the Karp/Hastad hardness facts or machine-level polynomial-time transfer; this remains a known partial external complexity boundary.

assumption_external_approximation_set_pa.reason: The source theorem uses external inapproximability facts and randomized complexity consequences; the Lean endpoint is intentionally conditional on that reusable complexity library boundary, so this is not a source caveat.
assumption_external_approximation_set_pa.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 510-525, Theorem 6.1 and cited Karp/Hastad route

hexternal : ∀ wspSolver, weightedSetPack.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 510-525: a polynomial-time approximation guarantee at the stated factor would imply `NP = ZPP`.
hexternal : ∀ wspSolver, weightedSetPack.reason: The source states the inapproximability consequence, but the current Lean library does not derive the cited hardness and randomized-complexity facts from a machine model; this remains a known partial external complexity boundary.

assumption_theorem7_optimal_allocation_f.reason: Theorem 7.2 compares the greedy allocation with `OP`, the optimal solution; pairwise disjoint desired bundles are the finite single-minded representation of OP feasibility.

hoptimal_disjoint : PairwiseDisjointDesi.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 657-658: `Let OP be the optimal solution`.
hoptimal_disjoint : PairwiseDisjointDesi.reason: An optimal allocation in the single-minded allocation problem is feasible; the Lean predicate states the pairwise-disjoint feasibility part explicitly.

assumption_theorem7_optimal_bidders_in_o.reason: Theorem 7.2 proves the greedy algorithm by processing the sorted list of bids; the Lean explicit-order theorem records that the optimal bidders under comparison are present in that order.
assumption_theorem7_optimal_bidders_in_o.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 593-602 and 657-666, greedy list and OP setup

hoptimal_order : ∀ i, i ∈ optimal → i ∈.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 593-602: bids are sorted into a list and processed in order; lines 657-666 introduce OP.
hoptimal_order : ∀ i, i ∈ optimal → i ∈.reason: The source greedy proof assumes the sorted bid list contains the bids being compared; Lean makes that list-membership condition explicit for an arbitrary order theorem.

assumption_lemma9_denied_bidder_case.reason: Lemma 9.2 is explicitly the denied-bidder case.

hdeny : i ∉ M.accepted reports.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 920-925: `a bidder whose bid is denied has utility zero`.
hdeny : i ∉ M.accepted reports.reason: The Lean premise is exactly the denied-bidder case split in Lemma 9.2.

assumption_lemma9_nonnegative_value_devi.reason: The single-minded source domain uses nonnegative bid values; Lemma 9.4 ranges over source-domain value declarations.

hv' : 0 ≤ v'.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 462-464: single-minded bids have nonnegative values; lines 934-944 state the value-only deviation.
hv' : 0 ≤ v'.reason: The alternative value declaration remains in the source single-minded declaration domain, whose values are nonnegative.

assumption_lemma9_nonnegative_critical_v.reason: Lemmas 9.3--9.6 are stated for mechanisms whose payments are their critical values, with the infinite critical-value branch represented explicitly in Lean. The concrete greedy mechanism later constructs this certificate from its source branch data.

C : M.NonnegativeCriticalValueWithInfini.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 900-977: critical-value payments and the infinite/no-winning critical branch used in Lemmas 9.3--9.6
C : M.NonnegativeCriticalValueWithInfini.reason: The certificate is the Lean packaging of the source critical-price axioms on nonempty nonnegative single-minded declarations; it is not an external complexity boundary.

assumption_lemma9_finite_large_threshold.reason: Lemma 9.5 is the finite critical-price comparison case for a granted larger desired set.

hLarge : C.threshold reports i sLarge =.source_location: LOS02CombinatorialAuctions.txt lines 949-965: Lemma 9.5 compares the price paid for the original bid with the price for the alternative set.
hLarge : C.threshold reports i sLarge =.reason: The Lean premise selects the finite-threshold branch of the source critical-price comparison.
